attempted_job.description: Butler claims that, strictly speaking, the only thing that anyone has a right to is:
Find the solutions y of the differential equation y'=(t^2+3y^2)/2ty with y(1) = 1. What is y(2)?

The diffusion coefficient of a particular kind of t-RNA molecule is $D=1.0 \times 10^{-11} \mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ in the medium of a cell interior. How long does it take molecules produced in the cell nucleus to reach the walls of the cell at a distance $1.0 \mu \mathrm{m}$, corresponding to the radius of the cell?
What factors within the individual are the basis for being able to either withstand conflict or develop neuroses?
options: Childhood upbringing and current employment status
Marital status and number of children
Amount of conflict a person can handle and life experiences
attempted_job.correct_answer: Amount of conflict a person can handle and life experiences

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
"We found that not only was it a civil war, an effort by a people who had for years been seeking their liberation from any colonial influence whatsoever, but also we found that the Vietnamese whom we had enthusiastically molded after our own image were hard put to take up the fight against the threat we were supposedly saving them from.
"We found most people didn't even know the difference between communism and democracy. They only wanted to work in rice paddies without helicopters strafing them and bombs with napalm burning their villages and tearing their country apart. They wanted everything to do with the war, particularly with this foreign presence of the United States of America, to leave them alone in peace, and they practiced the art of survival by siding with whichever military force was present at a particular time, be it Viet Cong, North Vietnamese or American."
John Kerry, 1971
Which of the following pairs of words best characterizes the differences between the approaches of Presidents Lyndon Johnson (1963–1969) and Richard Nixon (1969–1974) to the Vietnam conflict?

A 57-year-old man comes to the emergency department because of cramping in his hands and feet and numbness and tingling around his lips and in his fingers; these symptoms occurred intermittently for 6 months but have been progressively severe during the past 2 weeks. He also has had a 13-kg (30-lb) weight loss and bulky, foul-smelling stools that do not flush easily. He has a 10-year history of drinking 8 to 10 beers daily. He has been hospitalized twice for severe abdominal pain 4 and 6 years ago. His pulse is 80/min, and blood pressure is 105/65 mm Hg. He appears cachectic and chronically ill. The abdomen is nontender. Deep tendon reflexes are 4+ bilaterally. Chvostek and Trousseau signs are present. His serum calcium concentration is 6.5 mg/dL. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
The principle Arthur calls "the greater moral evil rule"says that
options: morality itself is a great evil.
people should always strive to commit the lesser evil, even if it results in personal gain.
the person who commits the greater evil is the one who is morally responsible for the situation.
people are entitled to keep their earnings only if there is no way for them to prevent a greater evil by giving them away.
people are morally obligated to share their wealth if it can prevent a greater evil.
being rich is a great moral evil, and as such we should all strive to live on a moderate income.
the greater moral evil rule is a concept which states that it's always wrong to do evil, regardless of the circumstances.
two moral evils do not make a moral good.
people are allowed to do a smaller evil to prevent a greater one.
the concept of evil is subjective and varies from person to person.
attempted_job.correct_answer: people are entitled to keep their earnings only if there is no way for them to prevent a greater evil by giving them away.

attempted_job.description: Radiation from an X-ray source consists of two components of wavelengths $154.433 \mathrm{pm}$ and $154.051 \mathrm{pm}$. Calculate the difference in glancing angles $(2 \theta)$ of the diffraction lines arising from the two components in a diffraction pattern from planes of separation $77.8 \mathrm{pm}$.
A monopsonist is identified by one of the following:
options: A wage payment lower than the average revenue product of labor.
Employment level lower than that of a competitive labor market.
A single buyer of labor services.
Employment level greater than that of a competitive labor market.
A wage payment lower than the marginal revenue product of labor.
A wage payment equal to that of a competitive labor market.
A wage payment higher than that of a competitive labor market.
A multiple seller of labor services.
Employment level equal to that of a competitive labor market.
A single seller of labor services.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A wage payment lower than the marginal revenue product of labor.

Which of the following statements about protein structure is correct?
options: Cross-links formed by reduction of -SH groups of cysteine to form disulphide bridges render the amino acid unavailable to digestion.
Cross-links between the ϵ-amino group of lysine and the carboxyl group of aspartate render the amino acid(s) unavailable for digestion.
All proteins have the same secondary structure
The arrangement of amino acids in a protein is its secondary structure
The sequence of amino acids in a protein is its quaternary structure
All proteins have the same tertiary structure
Cross-links between the ϵ-amino group of lysine and the carboxyl group of glutamate render the amino acid(s) unavailable for digestion.
All proteins have the same primary structure
Cross-links formed by oxidation of -SH groups of cysteine to form disulphide bridges render the amino acid unavailable to digestion.
The sequence of amino acids in a protein is its tertiary structure
attempted_job.correct_answer: Cross-links between the ϵ-amino group of lysine and the carboxyl group of glutamate render the amino acid(s) unavailable for digestion.

What is the output of the following program? PROGRAM TEST (output); VAR I, J, K, COUNT : integer; BEGIN I: = 1; J: = 2; K: = 3; count: = 1; write1n (I div J, K mod J); while count < = 3 DO BEGIN If I < J then If K > J then If (K > I) or (I = J) then write1n (I) ELSE write1n (J) ELSE write1n (K); CASE COUNT OF 1: J: = 0; 2: BEGIN K: = K - 1; J: = J + K END; 3:I: = 2 \textasteriskcentered I; END; count : = count + 1; END {while count........} END.
How many pairs of (a, b) can we substitute for a and b in 30a0b03 so that the resulting integer is divisible by 13?

This question refers to the following information.
"Since the foundations of the American commonwealth were laid in colonial times over 300 years ago, vigorous complaint and more or less bitter persecution have been aimed at newcomers to our shores. Also the congressional reports of about 1840 are full of abuse of English, Scotch, Welsh immigrants as paupers, criminals, and so forth. Old citizens in Detroit of Irish and German descent have told me of the fierce tirades and propaganda directed against the great waves of Irish and Germans who came over from 1840 on for a few decades to escape civil, racial, and religious persecution in their native lands. The "Know-Nothings," lineal ancestors of the Ku-Klux Klan, bitterly denounced the Irish and Germans as mongrels, scum, foreigners, and a menace to our institutions, much as other great branches of the Caucasian race of glorious history and antecedents are berated to-day….But to-day it is the Italians, Spanish, Poles, Jews, Greeks, Russians, Balkanians, and so forth, who are the racial lepers…..In this bill we find racial discrimination at its worst—a deliberate attempt to go back 84 years in our census taken every 10 years so that a blow may be aimed at peoples of eastern and southern Europe, particularly at our recent allies in the Great War—Poland and Italy."
Robert H. Clancy, Congressional Record, 68th Congress, 1st Session, 1924
Which one of the following legislative acts is most closely an example of nativism?
options: The Immigration and Nationality Act of 1965
The Alien and Sedition Acts of 1798
attempted_job.correct_answer: The Alien and Sedition Acts of 1798

attempted_job.description: A 57-year-old man comes to the emergency department because of cramping in his hands and feet and numbness and tingling around his lips and in his fingers; these symptoms occurred intermittently for 6 months but have been progressively severe during the past 2 weeks. He also has had a 13-kg (30-lb) weight loss and bulky, foul-smelling stools that do not flush easily. He has a 10-year history of drinking 8 to 10 beers daily. He has been hospitalized twice for severe abdominal pain 4 and 6 years ago. His pulse is 80/min, and blood pressure is 105/65 mm Hg. He appears cachectic and chronically ill. The abdomen is nontender. Deep tendon reflexes are 4+ bilaterally. Chvostek and Trousseau signs are present. His serum calcium concentration is 6.5 mg/dL. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

An owner entered into a written contract with a landscaper whereby the landscaper promised to landscape the exterior of the owner's house for the sum of $5,000. According to their agreement, the owner was to pay the money to the landscaper's son. The landscaper intended to have the $5,000 given to his son as a graduation present. After the landscaper completed the job, the landscaper requested that the owner pay the $5,000 to him instead of to his son. The owner paid the landscaper. The next day, the son learned of the contract between the landscaper and the owner, as well as the payment to his father. In an action by the son against the owner for $5,000, the plaintiff will most likely
options: prevail, because the written contract between the owner and the landscaper operated as a valid assignment to the son.
prevail, because the son was the intended beneficiary under the terms of the written contract between the owner and the landscaper.
not prevail, because the son did not give any consideration.
prevail, because the owner had a duty to honor the original agreement.
not prevail, because the landscaper, as the original contractor, had the right to alter the terms of payment.
not prevail, because the son was not a party to the original contract.
not prevail, because the son was not directly involved in the contractual agreement.
prevail, because the owner breached the contract by not paying the son.
prevail, because the son was a third party beneficiary of the contract.
not prevail, because the owner and the landscaper effectively modified their agreement, thereby depriving the son of any rights he may have had.
attempted_job.correct_answer: not prevail, because the owner and the landscaper effectively modified their agreement, thereby depriving the son of any rights he may have had.

Older adults are most likely to be involved in a traffic accident when they are
options: A passenger in a younger person's car

attempted_job.description: A gun fires a projectile of mass $10 \mathrm{~kg}$ of the type to which the curves of Figure 2-3 apply. The muzzle velocity is $140 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Through what angle must the barrel be elevated to hit a target on the same horizontal plane as the gun and $1000 \mathrm{~m}$ away? Compare the results with those for the case of no retardation.

Construct a complete truth table for the following argument. Then, using the truth table, determine whether the argument is valid or invalid. If the argument is invalid, choose an option which presents a counterexample. (There may be other counterexamples as well.)
~C ⊃ D
D ⊃ C / C
options: Invalid. Counterexample when C is true and D is false
Invalid. Counterexample when D is false and C is true
Invalid. Counterexample when C and D are true
Invalid. Counterexample when C and D are both false
Invalid. Counterexample when C is false and D is true
Invalid. Counterexample when D is true and C is false
Invalid. Counterexample when D and C are both true

attempted_job.description: A grantor executed a deed by which he conveyed his apartment building for a consideration of one dollar, receipt of which was acknowledged, "to my son for life, then to my uncle for life, but if my uncle moves to another state, to my brother for the life of my uncle, then to the heirs of my uncle if my uncle does not move to another state, and to the heirs of my brother if my uncle does move to another state. " This deed was promptly recorded. During the son's lifetime, the brother's interest may best be described as a (an)
options: vested executory interest pur autre vie.
contingent executory interest pur autre vie.
shifting executory interest pur autre vie.
attempted_job.correct_answer: shifting executory interest pur autre vie.

attempted_job.description: Two men were told by the defendant that a medical devices company was now using the old warehouse on the river for storing its supply of defibrillators. Late one night, the two men broke into the warehouse and proceeded to load the large crates of defibrillators onto their truck. As they were leaving, one of the men inadvertently threw his cigarette butt into a refuse pile of old cardboard boxes and papers. Soon afterward, the refuse ignited into a small fire. Although the men had time to douse the fire without any danger to themselves, they did not do so. Instead, they fled. Meanwhile, the fire quickly spread, engulfing the entire warehouse in flames. If the defendant is later charged as a co-conspirator, in all likelihood he would be held responsible as
options: a principal in the second degree.
a principal in the first degree.

attempted_job.description: A buyer contracted in writing to purchase 1,000 gidgets (which are similar to widgets but are more popular because of their greater durability and- resiliency) from a manufacturer for a total contract price of $4,000. After this agreement was executed and before delivery, the buyer and the manufacturer agreed in a telephone conversation to change the quantity term from 1,000 to 800 gidgets. This modification is probably unenforceable, because
options: it violates the statute of frauds.
the buyer did not provide sufficient notice.
it was a verbal agreement, not a written one.
the parol evidence rule bars contradictory terms.
the manufacturer did not agree to the modification.
the original contract was in writing.
the price was not adjusted to reflect the change in quantity.
the telephone conversation was not recorded.
there was no written agreement to the modification.
attempted_job.correct_answer: it violates the statute of frauds.

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
Read the the following poem to answer questions.
Take up the White Man's burden—
Send forth the best ye breed—
Go bind your sons to exile
To serve your captives' need;
To wait in heavy harness,
On fluttered folk and wild—
Your new-caught, sullen peoples,
Half-devil and half-child.
...
Take up the White Man's burden—
The savage wars of peace—
Fill full the mouth of Famine
And bid the sickness cease;
And when your goal is nearest
The end for others sought…
Watch sloth and heathen Folly
Bring all your hopes to naught.
Rudyard Kipling, The White Man's Burden, 1899
Based on this poem, Kipling's conception of the British imperial mission could best be summed up as
options: an attempt to bring civilization to uncivilized peoples
a mission to educate and enlighten
a task that is doomed to fail
a profitable endeavor for the empire
a necessary evil for the good of the empire
a hopeless task with no purpose
a burden that should be willingly undertaken

attempted_job.description: NH_4HS(s) = NH_3(g) + H_2S(g) 0.06 mole of solid NH_4HS are introduced into a 2.4 liter flask at 20° C. (a) Calculate the per cent of the solid that will have decomposed into NH_3 and H_2S at equilibrium. (b)Calculate the number of moles of ammonia that would have to be added to the flask to reduce the decomposition of the solid to 1%.
What are the symptoms of a paranoiddisorder ?Discuss the threetypes of this disorder.
options: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.
Symptoms include hallucinations and mood swings. The three types are delusional disorder, shared delusion, and paranoia
Symptoms are delusions and hallucinations. Types include paranoid schizophrenia, paranoid personality disorder, and delusional disorder.
Symptoms are distrust and communication difficulties. The types include paranoid anxiety, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include obsessive thoughts and compulsive behaviors. The types are shared paranoia, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoia
Symptoms are anxiety and depression. Types include shared paranoia, paranoia, and paranoid personality disorder
The symptoms include chronic anxiety and mistrust. The types are paranoid schizophrenia, paranoia, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include extreme suspicion and social isolation. The types are delusional disorder, paranoia, and paranoid anxiety disorder.
Symptoms include persistent fear and social withdrawal. The types are paranoid psychosis, shared paranoid disorder, and paranoid depression.
The symptoms of paranoid disorder are delusions of grandeur and auditory hallucinations. The three types are paranoid personality disorder, paranoid schizophrenia, and paranoia.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.

attempted_job.description: A father brings his 22-year-old son to you for evaluation. Within the past 6 weeks, this formerly socially active young man has quit his job as a restaurant manager, has become withdrawn, and now spends most of his time in his room watching TV. He reports that he occasionally receives messages from aliens through the TV. Based on this information, which of the following is the most appropriate DSM-IV diagnosis

Nancy has the choice to spend one hour studying for an exam, mowing the lawn for one hour at a wage of $6, or babysitting her niece for one hour at a wage of $8. If we know that Nancy has chosen to study for the exam, which of the following is true?
options: The opportunity cost of studying is $6, which is equal to the benefit received from studying.
The opportunity cost of studying is $14, which is less than the benefit received from studying.
Nancy has no other options available besides studying, mowing the lawn, or babysitting.
Nancy values studying more than earning $6 from mowing the lawn.
Nancy is indifferent to both studying and mowing the lawn.
Nancy's behavior is irrational, since babysitting was clearly superior to all other options.
The benefit received from studying is less than the opportunity cost of $8.
The benefit received from studying is greater than the opportunity cost of $8.
The opportunity cost of studying is $8, but the benefit of studying is unmeasurable.
Nancy prefers babysitting to studying but chose to study instead.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The benefit received from studying is greater than the opportunity cost of $8.

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
"With 78 percent of the Union electorate casting ballots, Lincoln was reelected in an Electoral College landslide, 212 to McClellan's 21. The 55% popular vote for the president was the third largest in the nineteenth century, surpassed only by Jackson's first victory in 1828 and Grant's reelection in 1872. McClellan won only New Jersey, Delaware, and Kentucky. Republicans drew support from native-born farmers, skilled and professional workers, those of New England descent, younger voters, and military personnel. Democrats were strongest in the cities and among Irish- and German-Americans (the most populous immigrant groups). It has been estimated that Lincoln received 78% of the vote of Union soldiers and sailors. The figure was not necessary for his reelection, but was perhaps the margin of victory in a few close states and, more importantly, of great symbolic value. Republicans also gained seats in Congress to retain unassailable control, 149 to 42 in the House and 42 to 10 in the Senate; took back several state legislatures; and lost only the governorship of New Jersey (McClellan's home state)."
Election of 1864, Harpweek.com
Which of the following conclusions is best supported by the excerpt above?
options: Republicans managed to gain control of Congress through the efforts of Lincoln to campaign on their behalf.
Lincoln won the Election of 1864 due to Union successes during the Civil War.
McClellan's home state, New Jersey, was the only state where he had significant support.
The number of votes cast in the Election of 1864 was one of the lowest in the nineteenth century.
The majority of the votes for Lincoln were from the younger demographic.
The majority of Lincoln's votes came from military personnel.
The Democrats' stronghold was in the rural areas and among farmers.
Lincoln received more votes in the heavily populated states of the North, while McClellan won more sparsely populated states.
The Emancipation Proclamation mobilized black voters in the South to vote for Lincoln in the Election of 1864.
Lincoln's victory was primarily due to the support of the immigrant population.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Lincoln received more votes in the heavily populated states of the North, while McClellan won more sparsely populated states.

attempted_job.description: Young Tina had never seen the space shuttle until her parents pointed out a picture of it ready to launch. When she next saw a picture of it flying, she had difficulty recognizing it. Which concept best explains this problem?

A teenage male was on trial on charges of illegally entering an owner's property and stealing a car. The witness can only confirm for the prosecution that a car was stolen, the model and type of car, and that it was missing from the property on a certain date. The defense asks witness "isn't it possible that my client did not steal the car, that someone else did it, and that the police are lying about the charges against him? The prosecution objected to the question. Will the court likely sustain the objection?
options: No, as it is essential for the defense to create reasonable doubt in the minds of the jury.
Yes, because the question is not directly related to the charges against the defendant.
Yes, because this is an objectionable question based on pure speculation.
No, because the defense is merely suggesting a possibility, not stating it as fact.
No, because the defense has the right to question every aspect of the prosecution's case.
Yes, because it is not permissible to question the credibility of the police without substantial supporting evidence.
No, questioning about the possibility of other perpetrators, or about police misconduct, is always permissible.
No, because the witness may know something that he is not revealing and for that reason, a cross-examiner is given wide latitude in questioning.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Yes, because this is an objectionable question based on pure speculation.

attempted_job.description: A 23-year-old woman comes to the physician for genetic counseling prior to conception. Her brother and maternal uncle had Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) and died at the ages of 28 and 17 years, respectively. Genetic analysis was not performed on either relative prior to death. Serum studies show a muscle creatine kinase concentration of 120 U/L (N=22– 198). The patient's 50-year-old mother has a serum muscle creatine kinase concentration of 300 U/L. Which of the following is the most appropriate assessment of this patient's carrier status for this disease?
options: The patient is not a carrier of the DMD as her muscle creatine kinase concentration is within the normal range
The patient's mother is a carrier, hence the patient is definitely a carrier too
The patient is a carrier of the disease based on her family history of DMD
The patient's DMD carrier status is uncertain due to lack of genetic analysis on her deceased relatives.
The patient has a 25% risk of having a child with DMD
The patient is a carrier of the disease based on her mother's elevated creatine kinase concentration
The patient's DMD carrier status is certain due to maternal transmission
The patient has a 50% risk of having a child with DMD
The patient's DMD carrier status is uncertain because of random X inactivation
The patient is not a carrier of the DMD based on her normal creatine kinase concentration
attempted_job.correct_answer: The patient's DMD carrier status is uncertain due to lack of genetic analysis on her deceased relatives.

attempted_job.description: A student decides that, having already spent three hours studying for an exam, she should spend one more hour studying for the same exam. Which of the following is most likely true?
options: The marginal benefit of the third hour was likely greater than the marginal benefit of the fourth hour.
The marginal cost of the third hour was likely greater than the marginal cost of the fourth hour.
The marginal benefit of the fourth hour is at least as great as the marginal cost of the fourth hour.
The marginal benefit of the fourth hour is certainly less than the marginal cost of the fourth hour.
The marginal cost of the fourth hour is at least as great as the marginal benefit of the fourth hour.
The marginal cost of the fourth hour is likely less than the marginal cost of the third hour.
The marginal benefit of the fourth hour is certainly more than the marginal cost of the fourth hour.
Without knowing the student’s opportunity cost of studying, we have no way of knowing whether or not her marginal benefits outweigh her marginal costs.
Without knowing the student's other commitments, we cannot determine the marginal cost or benefit of the fourth hour.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The marginal benefit of the fourth hour is at least as great as the marginal cost of the fourth hour.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following statements about the DNA in one of your liver cells is correct?

The DNA in your liver cells is only used for the production of liver enzymes.
The DNA in your liver cells is different from the DNA in your other cells.
Your liver cells contain the same DNA as your red blood cells, but not your white blood cells.
Most of the DNA in your liver cells will be transcribed but not translated.
The DNA in your liver cells only contains genes for liver-specific functions.
Your liver cells contain less DNA than your white blood cells.
Most of the DNA in your liver cells will be transcribed in the nucleus and translated in the cytoplasm.
DNA in your liver cells is primarily transcribed in the cytoplasm and translated in the nucleus.
Your liver cells contain the same DNA as your white blood cells.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Your liver cells contain the same DNA as your white blood cells.

An owner entered into a written contract with a landscaper whereby the landscaper promised to landscape the exterior of the owner's house for the sum of $5,000. According to their agreement, the owner was to pay the money to the landscaper's son. The landscaper intended to have the $5,000 given to his son as a graduation present. After the landscaper completed the job, the landscaper requested that the owner pay the $5,000 to him instead of to his son. The owner paid the landscaper. The next day, the son learned of the contract between the landscaper and the owner, as well as the payment to his father. In an action by the son against the owner for $5,000, the plaintiff will most likely
options: prevail, because the written contract between the owner and the landscaper operated as a valid assignment to the son.
prevail, because the son was the intended beneficiary under the terms of the written contract between the owner and the landscaper.
not prevail, because the son did not give any consideration.
prevail, because the owner had a duty to honor the original agreement.
not prevail, because the landscaper, as the original contractor, had the right to alter the terms of payment.
not prevail, because the son was not a party to the original contract.
not prevail, because the son was not directly involved in the contractual agreement.
prevail, because the owner breached the contract by not paying the son.
prevail, because the son was a third party beneficiary of the contract.
not prevail, because the owner and the landscaper effectively modified their agreement, thereby depriving the son of any rights he may have had.
attempted_job.correct_answer: not prevail, because the owner and the landscaper effectively modified their agreement, thereby depriving the son of any rights he may have had.

attempted_job.description: A 23-year-old woman comes to the physician for genetic counseling prior to conception. Her brother and maternal uncle had Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) and died at the ages of 28 and 17 years, respectively. Genetic analysis was not performed on either relative prior to death. Which of the following is the most appropriate assessment of this patient's carrier status for this disease?
options: The patient is not a carrier of the DMD as her muscle creatine kinase concentration is within the normal range
The patient's mother is a carrier, hence the patient is definitely a carrier too
The patient is a carrier of the disease based on her family history of DMD
The patient's DMD carrier status is uncertain due to lack of genetic analysis on her deceased relatives.
The patient has a 25% risk of having a child with DMD
The patient is a carrier of the disease based on her mother's elevated creatine kinase concentration
The patient's DMD carrier status is certain due to maternal transmission
The patient has a 50% risk of having a child with DMD
The patient's DMD carrier status is uncertain because of random X inactivation
The patient is not a carrier of the DMD based on her normal creatine kinase concentration
attempted_job.correct_answer: The patient's DMD carrier status is uncertain due to lack of genetic analysis on her deceased relatives.

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
"Since the foundations of the American commonwealth were laid in colonial times over 300 years ago, vigorous complaint and more or less bitter persecution have been aimed at newcomers to our shores. Also the congressional reports of about 1840 are full of abuse of English, Scotch, Welsh immigrants as paupers, criminals, and so forth. Old citizens in Detroit of Irish and German descent have told me of the fierce tirades and propaganda directed against the great waves of Irish and Germans who came over from 1840 on for a few decades to escape civil, racial, and religious persecution in their native lands. The "Know-Nothings," lineal ancestors of the Ku-Klux Klan, bitterly denounced the Irish and Germans as mongrels, scum, foreigners, and a menace to our institutions, much as other great branches of the Caucasian race of glorious history and antecedents are berated to-day….But to-day it is the Italians, Spanish, Poles, Jews, Greeks, Russians, Balkanians, and so forth, who are the racial lepers…..In this bill we find racial discrimination at its worst—a deliberate attempt to go back 84 years in our census taken every 10 years so that a blow may be aimed at peoples of eastern and southern Europe, particularly at our recent allies in the Great War—Poland and Italy."
Robert H. Clancy, Congressional Record, 68th Congress, 1st Session, 1924
Which one of the following legislative acts is most closely an example of nativism?
options: The Immigration and Nationality Act of 1965
The Alien and Sedition Acts of 1798
attempted_job.correct_answer: The Alien and Sedition Acts of 1798

attempted_job.description: Have studies on learning supported the Incremental or the One-trial Theory of learning?
options: Incremental Theory is supported in cognitive tasks, while One-trial Theory is supported in motor tasks.
The Incremental Theory is universally supported, while the One-trial Theory has been completely discredited.
Studies support the One-trial Theory in the short term, but the Incremental Theory in the long term.
Studies support a hybrid model that combines elements of both theories.
One-trial Theory is supported for simple tasks, while Incremental Theory is for complex tasks.
Neither theories of learning are correct.
Both theories of learning are correct, depending on the context.
The Incremental Theory is only supported in animal studies, while the One-trial Theory is supported in human studies.
Only One-trial Theory of learning is supported.
Only Incremental Theory of learning is supported.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Both theories of learning are correct, depending on the context.

can measure the patient's pain with medical instruments.
can use the information to prescribe and administer the least possible amount of pain relief.
attempted_job.correct_answer: do not possess an objective way of assessing pain.

This question refers to the following information.
"We found that not only was it a civil war, an effort by a people who had for years been seeking their liberation from any colonial influence whatsoever, but also we found that the Vietnamese whom we had enthusiastically molded after our own image were hard put to take up the fight against the threat we were supposedly saving them from.
"We found most people didn't even know the difference between communism and democracy. They only wanted to work in rice paddies without helicopters strafing them and bombs with napalm burning their villages and tearing their country apart. They wanted everything to do with the war, particularly with this foreign presence of the United States of America, to leave them alone in peace, and they practiced the art of survival by siding with whichever military force was present at a particular time, be it Viet Cong, North Vietnamese or American."
John Kerry, 1971

attempted_job.description: A defendant met her friend at the electronics store where the friend worked. Unbeknownst to the defendant, her friend had taken a music player from the display case and stuffed it in her bag earlier in the day. Knowing that employees are subject to search when they leave the store, the friend surreptitiously put the music player into the defendant's bag. The defendant and her friend proceeded to a local tavern and drank shots of tequila. The friend became intoxicated and forgot to retrieve the stolen music player from the defendant's bag. After leaving the tavern, the defendant proceeded home. Upon opening her bag, she was surprised to see the music player. Believing that the music player had accidentally fallen into her bag at the bar, the defendant planned to return it the next day. The following day, however, the defendant changed her mind and decided to keep it. The defendant is guilty of

attempted_job.correct_answer: neither receiving stolen property nor larceny.
